REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, HOWARD OLSEN'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (FOR HANS WILMS). NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. COUNSEL DID NOT ATTACH A DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION. "THE MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL MUST BE ACCOMPANIED BY A DECLARATION ON THE DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF ATTORNEY'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL-CIVIL (FORM MC-052). THE DECLARATION MUST STATE IN GENERAL TERMS AND WITHOUT COMPROMISING THE CONFIDENTIALITY OF THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP WHY A MOTION UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 284(2) IS BROUGHT INSTEAD OF FILING A CONSENT UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 284(1)." (CRC 3.1362(C)). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Aug-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: HOWARD OLSEN'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (FOR HANS WILMS)

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Counsel did not attach a declaration in support of the motion. "The motion to be relieved as counsel must be accompanied by a declaration on the Declaration in Support of Attorney's Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel-Civil (form MC-052). The declaration must state in general terms and without compromising the confidentiality of the attorney-client relationship why a motion under Code of Civil Procedure section 284(2) is brought instead of filing a consent under Code of Civil Procedure section 284(1)." (CRC 3.1362(c)). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
